sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Ongoing management of psoriatic arthritis.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports ongoing management of psoriatic arthritis. She states that she's doing good, thank you. She reports that she's been taking one prednisone pill and that seems to be enough. She mentions a couple of days where she took an extra one because there was a little extra pain in her feet. She also reports that she has a desk job and when she's off and moving around a lot, her feet tend to hurt more. The patient reports that she took methotrexate, but she had a COVID shot and the letter said it could interfere with the vaccine, so she didn't take it the week she got her first shot. She felt absolutely horrible until she took it again a week later. The patient reports experiencing bruising and doesn't know where it's coming from. She also reports nail ridges and that they've been present for a long time. She has ongoing management of psoriatic arthritis.
Findings
Examination reveals good range of motion in the toes. Nail ridges are present. The patient reports pain in the left hand and feet. The patient reports pain.
Assessment
The patient has psoriatic arthritis. The patient is taking prednisone and methotrexate.
Plan
We will discontinue prednisone and continue with methotrexate. Make sure to schedule an appointment for blood work. I'll see you in three months. ,
